members.name: Snack Line Shuffle
members.grade_band_notes: K-1 core; Grade 2+ layers gated as ceiling. Formally locked in the approved concept packet at concepts/snack-line-shuffle/concept.md.
members.primary_standard_note: Formally claimed in the approved concept packet at concepts/snack-line-shuffle/concept.md. This is the only entry in this registry with a formal alignment claim backed by a concepts/ packet.

notes: First K-2 family in the portfolio. First proof case for the sequence_and_order interaction type. Family coverage must not be marked as fully proven until a P1 prototype exists and passes its Definition of Done.

members.name: Bakery Rush
members.grade_band_notes: No formal grade-band lock in the repo. README benchmark table informally refers to 'K-2, addition', but this is benchmark-case language, not a formal packet.
members.primary_standard_note: No formal alignment packet. README benchmark table and docs/concept_inputs/top_5_math_concepts_claude_packet.md informally refer to 'addition within 20', but this is benchmark-case language, not a formal CCSS alignment. Backfilling a formal concepts/bakery/ packet is explicitly deferred per the locked cleanup decisions.
members.concept_source_of_truth_note: No concepts/bakery/ folder exists. Concept description lives informally in docs/concept_inputs/top_5_math_concepts_claude_packet.md and docs/concept_inputs/brain_games_concept_library_by_subject.md.

coverage_status: active review build exists; no release; no formal concept packet; no formal curriculum alignment
notes: Family 'Quantity and Fulfillment' is defined in docs/concept_inputs/strategy/family_map_math.md as Family 1, with Bakery Rush explicitly named as a strongest current concept in that family. Interaction type 'combine_and_build' is listed under example_games in orchestrator_v3/policies/interaction_types.yaml. Neither of those references constitutes a formal alignment packet or a formal grade-band lock, so primary_standard and grade_band are null at the member level.

pseudo_family_note: This is NOT a real family. It is a structural holding area for games that exist in the repo but do not yet have a formal family assignment in docs/concept_inputs/strategy/family_map_math.md. Entries here should be moved to their proper family once formal placement is decided. Per-member interaction_type values reflect what is (or is not) currently documented in orchestrator_v3/policies/interaction_types.yaml. Inclusion here does not imply a family assignment, a curriculum alignment, or a grade-band lock.

members.name: Fire Dispatch
members.grade_band_notes: No formal grade-band lock in the repo. README benchmark table informally refers to 'grades 4-6, multiplication'.
members.primary_standard_note: No formal alignment packet. README benchmark table informally refers to 'multiplication', but the actual game mechanic is subset-sum selection from a fixed pool of truck capacities (additive decomposition), not multiplication.
members.concept_source_of_truth_note: No concepts/fire/ folder exists. Concept description lives informally in docs/concept_inputs/top_5_math_concepts_claude_packet.md.
members.family_placement_note: Not formally placed in docs/concept_inputs/strategy/family_map_math.md. No documented best-fit family.

members.name: Unit Circle Pizza Lab
members.grade_band_notes: No formal grade-band lock in the repo. README benchmark table informally refers to 'high school, trigonometry'.
members.primary_standard_note: No formal alignment packet. README benchmark table informally refers to 'trigonometry'. The game mechanic covers unit circle angles and coordinates but no specific CCSS code is formally claimed.
members.concept_source_of_truth_note: No concepts/unitcircle/ folder exists. Concept description lives informally in docs/concept_inputs/top_5_math_concepts_claude_packet.md.
members.family_placement_note: Not formally placed in docs/concept_inputs/strategy/family_map_math.md. Closest documented adjacency is Family 3 (Coordinate Navigation), but that family's examples are Cartesian grid games; Unit Circle is angular/trigonometric.

members.name: Watering Hole Count
members.grade_band_notes: No formal grade-band lock in the repo. In-file header comment informally refers to ages 4-6 (Pre-K to Grade 1).
members.primary_standard_note: No formal alignment packet. In-file header comment informally refers to 'counting / cardinality' without a specific CCSS code.
members.interaction_type_note: Enumerate-and-report / count-and-verify is not currently represented in the canonical interaction taxonomy in orchestrator_v3/policies/interaction_types.yaml. Not a clean match for any of: combine_and_build, route_and_dispatch, allocate_and_balance, transform_and_manipulate, navigate_and_position, sequence_and_predict, sequence_and_order.
members.concept_source_of_truth_note: No concepts/counting/ folder exists. Concept description lives in artifacts/learning_captures/2026-04-07-counting-concept-lane.md and 2026-04-07-counting-p0p1-implementation-plan.md (historical, not rewritten).
members.family_placement_note: Not formally placed. No documented family fits counting/cardinality as a distinct cognitive act.

members.name: Echo Heist
members.grade_band_notes: No formal grade-band lock in the repo and no informal claim either.
members.primary_standard_note: No formal alignment packet. Multi-topic content across districts 1-3 (addition, subtraction, rounding, percents, fractions, rates, expected value); no single-standard mapping.
members.interaction_type_note: Hybrid game. The math subloop is single-expression compute-and-answer, which is not represented in the canonical taxonomy. The outer loop is stealth navigation + class abilities, which is game-mechanical rather than math-interactive.
members.concept_source_of_truth_note: No concepts/echo-heist/ folder exists. Concept description lives in docs/concept_inputs/Echo Heist Game Concept.md (1750 lines) with a duplicate of a different export at reviews/echo-heist/current/Echo Heist Game Concept.md (1407 lines). Duplicate cleanup is tracked as Cleanup Step 2, not done yet.
members.family_placement_note: Not formally placed. No documented family fits this concept.

members.name: Power Grid Operator
members.grade_band_notes: No formal grade-band lock in the repo and no informal claim either.
members.primary_standard_note: No formal alignment packet. Game mechanic is single-step additive equation (current + x = demand), consistent with early missing-addend work, but no explicit CCSS standard is claimed anywhere in the repo.
members.interaction_type_note: Core act is solving a one-step equation (missing addend). Closest documented adjacency: combine_and_build (additive reasoning), but the player works backward from a target rather than accumulating toward it. No formal placement.
members.concept_source_of_truth_note: No concepts/power-grid/ folder exists. Concept lives inline in the review build with no external packet.
members.family_placement_note: Not formally placed. No documented family fits this concept.

coverage_status: placeholder holding area; not a real family
notes: Structural placeholder for games that exist in the repo but lack formal family assignment. Each member lists its own per-field provenance. Entries should be migrated to their proper family object when formal placement is documented in family_map_math.md or orchestrator_v3/policies/interaction_types.yaml. Inclusion in this holding area does not constitute a family assignment, a curriculum alignment, or a grade-band lock.
